channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Magnetic Materials Breakthrough: D-Wave Quantum Achieves Quantum Supremacy in Complex Material Simulation
item.description: D-Wave Quantum announces a major breakthrough in the simulation of complex magnetic materials, achieving quantum supremacy on a problem that would take a classical supercomputer nearly a million years to solve.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Huawei at center of European Parliament bribery investigation
item.description: Huawei is at the center of an investigation into alleged bribery and corruption within the European Parliament. Belgian Federal Police raided 21 locations across Belgium and in Portugal on Thursday morning and made arrests in Belgium and France as part of the investigation.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: iOS 18.4 will give your iPhone a much-needed maps upgrade – but only if you're in the EU
item.description: Apple will soon let you change your default map app in iOS 18.4, but US users will miss out –for now.
item.dc:creator: alexblake.techradar@gmail.com (Alex Blake), TechRadar

item.title: Meta to start testing crowd-sourced fact-checking, based on X example, next week
item.description: Facebook and Instagram parent company Meta Platforms Inc. said Thursday it will begin testing its crowd-sourced fact-checking program, Community Notes, on March 18. It will initially based on a ratings system used by Elon Musk's X. Meta ended its fact-checking program in January.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: How DeepSeek innovated large language models
item.description: The release of DeepSeek roiled the world of generative AI last month, leaving engineers and developers wondering how the company achieved what it did, and how they might take advantage of the technology in their own technology stacks.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: NASA’s new AI satellites could revolutionize disaster response
item.description: Satellite-based disaster monitoring has been a slow and tedious process for decades. The process consists of capturing images, transmitting them back to Earth, and relying on human analysts to interpret the data.
item.dc:creator: Victor Dey, Fast Company

item.title: French publishers and authors sue Meta over copyright works used in AI training
item.description: French publishers and authors said Wednesday they're taking Meta to court, accusing the social media company of using their works without permission to train its artificial intelligence model.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump floats labeling violence against Tesla dealerships as domestic terrorism
item.description: President Trump hinted he would be open to labeling individuals who carry out violence at Tesla dealerships as “domestic terrorists” following a string of violent demonstrations at the electric vehicle company’s showrooms across the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Elon Musk claims X being targeted in 'massive cyberattack' as service goes down
item.description: Hours after a series of outages Monday that left X unavailable to thousands of users, Elon Musk claimed that the social media platform was being targeted in a “massive cyberattack.” “We get attacked every day, but this was done with a lot of resources," Musk claimed in a post.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE CHAPMAN and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Fortran, Delphi rise in Tiobe popularity index
item.description: Older programming languages such as Fortran and Delphi are making a comeback, based on the results of this month’s Tiobe index of language popularity. Tiobe attributes their rise to the need to maintain vital legacy systems still dependent on them.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Albania shuts down TikTok for a year amid concerns over violence among children
item.description: TIRANA, Albania (AP) — The Albanian Cabinet decided on Thursday to shut down TikTok for 12 months, blaming the popular video-sharing platform for inciting violence and bullying, especially among children.
item.dc:creator: LLAZAR SEMINI, Associated Press, KREX 5
